A ctx-taking provider with a prepare hook and no server or worker: compose
takes the application context so the provider receives it, run() supplies a
background context (there is no runtime to signal-shut-down), and the prepare
hook runs before returning.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

func main() {
	if err := run(); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
-- data/data.go --
package data

import "context"

type Store struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewStore(ctx context.Context) (*Store, error) { return &Store{}, nil }

//fabrik:hook prepare
func Migrate(ctx context.Context, s *Store) error { return nil }
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.
// Regenerate with: fabrik wire

package main

import (
	"context"

	"app/data"
)

func run() error {
	ctx := context.Background()
	// Providers
	dataStore, err := data.NewStore(ctx)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	// Prepare: after registration, before runtime start
	if err := data.Migrate(ctx, dataStore); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	return nil
}
